privileged_info_summary: All five chemicals in the shipping audit task have been independently verified against CAMEO Chemicals and official DOT/UN regulations. Ammonia Anhydrous (UN 1005) carries hazard class 2.2 (Non-Flammable Gas) for domestic U.S. shipping. Ammonia 25% Solution (UN 2672) is classified as Class 8 (Corrosive). Hydrogen Peroxide 50% (UN 2014) has dual hazards: Class 5.1 (Oxidizer) and Class 8 (Corrosive). Hydrogen Peroxide 15% (UN 2984) has Class 5.1 (Oxidizer) only, critically lacking the Class 8 (Corrosive) label that applies to higher concentrations. Nitric Acid Red Fuming (UN 2032) carries three hazard classes: 8 (Corrosive), 5.1 (Oxidizer), and 6.1 (Poison Inhalation Hazard). All UN numbers and hazard label classifications are accurate according to CAMEO Chemicals datasheets and the DOT Hazmat Table (49 CFR 172.101).
